title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
At Bredwood Manor, the butler Stollings informs Inspector Vance and Ellery Queen that he and Mrs. Baxter returned home via train and taxi around midnight after being driven to the Rockside Theatre by Fox. Stollings notes that this was the first time Mr. Braid had sent the staff and family away, as he had previously insisted on being alone in the evenings. During the investigation, Ellery examines a cigar box and confirms the contents are a special English blend preferred by Mr. Braid, who refuses to smoke American tobacco. Stollings also identifies a Neptune-head pipe as belonging to Mr. Braid, noting that the owner frequently rotates his pipes.

Officer Wain interrupts to report that a search of the scene on Oyster Island yielded no results, specifically failing to locate the missing head of the victim, who remains a headless corpse in the morgue. Vance explains that they have questioned local residents and everyone at the train station but found no evidence of a visitor. While the district attorney suggests a criminal would avoid the nearest station, Vance observes there are no tire tracks on the gravel to suggest a car was used. Ellery suggests the visitor may have arrived by boat via the channel, a lead Vance begins to pursue.

After an interrogation of an agitated Mrs. Baxter yields no new information regarding Mr. Braid's behavior or guests, the group moves to question Fox. They walk to a log hut in a grove of trees, where they encounter a tall, thin, muscular man who appears visibly shaken and fearful upon seeing them.
